name: Midlothian Country Club
about: Midlothian Country Club is a private club in the Chicago area featuring a historic, renovated 18-hole golf course designed by Herbert J. Tweedie with influences from Donald Ross and Raymond Hearn. The venue includes a modern Halfway House and Golf Simulator Lounge opened in September 2025, offering year-round practice and play with amenities like indoor and outdoor dining, a heated cigar patio, and a game room. The club provides a full experience with upscale dining, event spaces, and a family-friendly environment alongside golf activities for members and their guests.
why_golfers_like_it: Premium tech-friendly Halfway House and Simulator Lounge with year-round access
Championship-caliber course with 82 newly designed bunkers and lengthened tee boxes
Comprehensive practice facilities and professional coaching available

food_and_drink.notes: Upscale dining available in the 1898 Room, Championship Lounge, and Halfway Bar & Grille with outdoor patios
Full bar service available in dining areas and lounge spaces

faq.question: Do I need clubs?
faq.answer: The venue offers professional golf services and a pro shop for equipment. Simulator access likely requires personal clubs or rental options, though specifics are not detailed.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The club provides upscale dining options with indoor and outdoor spaces. External food and drink policies are not specified, but BYOB is not mentioned.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The course is designed for a typical round time of 3 hours and 45 minutes.

faq.question: Is it kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the club offers junior golf programs and family-friendly amenities, including a pool and events suitable for all ages.
